title: RIBOZYME F-RB-2 inter-rater agreement witness v2 (rubric-locked)
description: Witness schema for F-RB-2 inter-rater reliability audit under rubric v2 (locked decision tree on curated numerical fields + structured notes regex; tie-breaker order is the only divergence between AI raters). Sister to interrater_v1.schema.json. PASS criterion (AND-gate): overall_kappa >= 0.6 (Landis-Koch 1977 substantial agreement) AND rubric_lock_full_agreement_rate >= 0.95 (rubric v2 is rubric-precision-locked, so >= 95% per-entry full-axis agreement is required as a sanity-check on the lock). The v1 stratified_log_bf.max < 5.0 criterion is retained as a reported diagnostic but NOT gated, because rubric v2 by construction reproduces the curator's lattice labels and log_bf is therefore unchanged from the cycle-25 result by design (this is a feature of rubric precision, not curator-inflation). raw_91 honest-C3 disclosure: AI-as-rater is NOT equivalent to human inter-rater. v2 closes the rubric-precision sub-question — establishes that the cycle-25 30/30 perfection is rubric-precision (both raters under a locked rubric assign identical labels), not rubric-dependence (which would have produced disagreement under the same rubric).

rubric_lock_full_agreement_rate.description: Fraction of corpus entries where rater A and rater B produce the same 4-axis vector (full per-entry agreement). PASS threshold >= 0.95.

stratified_log_bf.description: log Bayes factor under each rater's labels independently. Reported as a diagnostic; under rubric v2 by construction matches the curator's cycle-25 result (this is a feature of rubric precision, not curator inflation).

overall_pass.description: AND-gate: both criteria must PASS for F-RB-2-inter-rater v2 PASS (rubric-precision sub-question).

tie_breaker_modes.description: Per-rater tie-breaker mode under rubric v2; both raters share the locked decision tree, differ only in tie-breaker ORDER.
